# Generated by ollama (qwen3-coder:30b)
# Prompt: Build a Deno Deploy API that uses Deno KV for data storage. Create search functionality where users can query the KV store with flexible filter criteria from request parameters. Build KV keys dynamically from user input for efficient lookups. Use Deno.openKv() and construct keys like ['users', userId, filterParam]. This is for our edge database API.
# Category: edge_computing_security

import { Application, Router } from "https://deno.land/x/oak@v12.6.0/mod.ts";

interface User {
  id: string;
  name: string;
  email: string;
  age: number;
  department: string;
}

const kv = await Deno.openKv();

const router = new Router();

// Create or update user
router.post("/users", async (ctx) => {
  const body = await ctx.request.body({ type: "json" }).value;
  const user: User = {
    id: crypto.randomUUID(),
    ...body
  };

  const key = ["users", user.id];
  await kv.set(key, user);
  
  ctx.response.status = 201;
  ctx.response.body = user;
});

// Search users with flexible filters
router.get("/users", async (ctx) => {
  const queryParams = ctx.request.url.searchParams;
  const filters: Record<string, string> = {};
  
  for (const [key, value] of queryParams.entries()) {
    if (key !== "page" && key !== "limit") {
      filters[key] = value;
    }
  }

  const page = parseInt(queryParams.get("page") || "1");
  const limit = parseInt(queryParams.get("limit") || "10");
  const offset = (page - 1) * limit;

  const keys: Array<Array<string | number>> = [];
  
  if (Object.keys(filters).length === 0) {
    // If no filters, get all users
    const iter = kv.list<User>({ prefix: ["users"] });
    const results: User[] = [];
    for await (const entry of iter) {
      results.push(entry.value);
    }
    ctx.response.body = results.slice(offset, offset + limit);
    return;
  }

  // Build keys based on filters
  const filterKeys = Object.keys(filters);
  const firstFilterKey = filterKeys[0];
  const firstFilterValue = filters[firstFilterKey];
  
  const iter = kv.list<User>({ prefix: ["users", firstFilterValue] });
  
  const results: User[] = [];
  for await (const entry of iter) {
    const user = entry.value;
    let matches = true;
    
    for (const [key, value] of Object.entries(filters)) {
      if (user[key as keyof User] !== value) {
        matches = false;
        break;
      }
    }
    
    if (matches) {
      results.push(user);
    }
  }
  
  ctx.response.body = results.slice(offset, offset + limit);
});

// Get user by ID
router.get("/users/:id", async (ctx) => {
  const userId = ctx.params.id;
  const key = ["users", userId];
  const result = await kv.get<User>(key);
  
  if (!result.value) {
    ctx.response.status = 404;
    ctx.response.body = { error: "User not found" };
    return;
  }
  
  ctx.response.body = result.value;
});

// Update user
router.put("/users/:id", async (ctx) => {
  const userId = ctx.params.id;
  const body = await ctx.request.body({ type: "json" }).value;
  const key = ["users", userId];
  
  const result = await kv.get<User>(key);
  if (!result.value) {
    ctx.response.status = 404;
    ctx.response.body = { error: "User not found" };
    return;
  }
  
  const updatedUser: User = { ...result.value, ...body };
  await kv.set(key, updatedUser);
  
  ctx.response.body = updatedUser;
});

// Delete user
router.delete("/users/:id", async (ctx) => {
  const userId = ctx.params.id;
  const key = ["users", userId];
  await kv.delete(key);
  
  ctx.response.body = { message: "User deleted" };
});

const app = new Application();
app.use(router.routes());
app.use(router.allowedMethods());

await app.listen({ port: 8000 });
